# UC-19.1.46 · OpenStack Galera / MariaDB Cluster Non-Primary or Split-Brain Indicators

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Availability, Reliability &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch the database machines that must agree on every change behind a private OpenStack cloud. Think of three judges: if the room splits in half, neither side has a real majority, so the system has to stop taking new written decisions, and many screens can fail at once. We flag when the cluster is no longer one healthy team so data experts can fix the foundation first.*

---

## Description

This use case is the **OpenStack control-plane Galera** detector. It uses **wsrep** semantics from a MariaDB / Galera cluster (often three nodes) that stores Keystone, Nova, Neutron, Cinder, Glance, Heat, and Placement metadata. The SPL fuses a **poll stream** of structured wsrep status ( **`wsrep_cluster_status`**, size, **UUID**, **flow control**, and **recv queue** ) with **error-log** text that names **gcomm** reconfigurations, **Non-Primary** transitions, and **SST/IST** cycles. It rolls up to **cluster_name** and **host**, derives an **expected_size** from the latest maximum **wsrep_cluster_size** seen in-window, flags **split-brain** when **dc(cluster_uuid)** exceeds one for the same cluster, and applies a **four-tier** **`severity_bucket`**: critical when a member is not **primary**, the UUIDs disagree, or the reporting host set is short while partition language is present; warning when the observed size lags the expected, multiple partition log hits stack up, or **flow/queue** signals show replication is choked; watch when donor/joiner language or a single light partition string appears in the log; otherwise ok. It does **not** replace Nova build/spawn or scheduler diagnostics (**UC-19.1.44**), RabbitMQ/AMQP churn (**UC-19.1.45**), or oVirt/Proxmox control-plane UCs; it is the **database-quorum and wsrep liveness** slice for the OpenStack data plane that everything else calls.

## Value

When the Galera layer loses **Primary** (non-primary members, size shrink, or **UUID** mismatch), the control plane often flips to **read-only**; OpenStack APIs then return **HTTP 500** across many services in parallel, which looks like a generic platform outage unless you can tell **database** from **message bus** from **compute**. This UC is built so a Splunk admin can see **whether the SQL quorum story is the root cause** versus an HAProxy, RabbitMQ, or Nova/libvirt path, and whether you are in a **governed SST/IST** window versus a **quorum** emergency. The business value is **MTTR and noise separation**: a correct early page to the DBA/infra owner who can decide on **pc.bootstrap**-class recovery, instead of a cross-team **Sev-1** where every app team is chasing unrelated symptoms. The narrative also helps executives understand why a **“small” database hiccup** can idle an entire private cloud, and when **manual cluster surgery** (bootstrap, re-seed) is the only path back versus **restarting a single API service**.

## Implementation

Create a `mysql:galera:wsrep` HEC/UF scripted poll plus **UF** `monitor://` of MariaDB error logs into `index=openstack` with a stable **`cluster_name`**. Run the `spl` as a five-minute saved search, suppress on `cluster_name,host` for 15m, and route `critical` to webhook paging and `warning` to email. Keep Option A and Option B **both** enabled in production: polls give you numeric truth at interval; error logs give you **gcomm** text between intervals.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- MariaDB 10.6+ with wsrep (Galera) for your OpenStack control plane, typically three odd-numbered nodes; larger clusters are possible but you must pin expected size in tuning.
- A Splunk index (here `openstack`) the forwarder and HEC can write; search role grants that index.
- A read-only monitoring user on port 3306 (client SQL) with `PROCESS` and `REPLICATION CLIENT` (or a minimal set your security team will approve) so `SHOW STATUS` is permitted; the poll uses the SQL port, not 4567 (wsrep replication).
- Network: 4567/4568 open between Galera members on the replication VLAN; the scripted poll only needs 3306 to localhost or the service VIP your site uses. Document cluster_name in `galera-monitoring` CMDB: separate clusters for different OpenStack subsystems (core vs ceilometer) must not share the same token.
- Time sync: NTP or chrony on all DB nodes; a skewed node poisons `wsrep` timestamps and your `_time` correlation with OpenStack service logs.

**Step 1 — Configure data collection**

You want Option A (poll) + Option B (error log) in serious estates: the poll is quantitative; the file log is qualitative (**gcomm** strings, Non-Primary text, and donor stack traces). Option C (syslog/SC4S) is optional when you already centralize mysqld via rsyslog through SC4S — set sourcetype routing in SC4S to land `syslog` (or a dedicated sourcetype) and rely on the same SPL’s `_raw` matches — but still deploy Option A for structured fields; do not only syslog unless you are prototyping.

Option A (recommended) — HEC JSON poll with `mysql:galera:wsrep`

Create `/etc/splunk-galera-creds.cnf` (mode 0600, owner root or the splunk user, group splunk as your policy dictates). No password is shown here; replace placeholders with a secret created out-of-band.

```ini
[client]
host=127.0.0.1
port=3306
user=monitoring_repl
password=REDACTED
socket=/var/run/mysqld/mysqld.sock
```

Install a small app, for example `TA_galera_wsrep`, under `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/TA_galera_wsrep/bin/`. The wrapper below runs MariaDB, emits one JSON line per run with explicit `_time`, and posts to a HEC token you pass via environment (set in a wrapper under `/etc/splunk-galera-hec.env`, mode 0600). The key idea: parse `SHOW STATUS WHERE Variable_name LIKE 'wsrep%'` (MariaDB) or the equivalent on your build into a flat object; include `cluster_name` (from a local `cluster.conf` the ship script reads) and `host` (FQDN of the machine).

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/TA_galera_wsrep/bin/galera_wsrep_hec.py
import json, os, subprocess, sys, time, urllib.request

def sh(cmd, env=None):
    p = subprocess.run(cmd, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, text=True, env=env, timeout=30)
    if p.returncode != 0:
        raise RuntimeError(p.stderr or "mysql failed")
    return p.stdout

def main():
    creds = os.environ.get("MY_CNF", "/etc/splunk-galera-creds.cnf")
    hec = os.environ["SPLUNK_HEC_URL"]
    tok = os.environ["SPLUNK_HEC_TOKEN"]
    cl = os.environ.get("GALERA_CLUSTER_NAME", "osctl-a")
    host = os.environ.get("SPLUNK_HOST", os.uname().nodename)
    # MariaDB: SHOW STATUS extends WHERE; fall back to LIKE if needed on older builds
    q = ("SELECT VARIABLE_NAME, VARIABLE_VALUE FROM information_schema.GLOBAL_STATUS "
         "WHERE VARIABLE_NAME LIKE 'wsrep%'" )
    cmd = ["mysql", f"--defaults-file={creds}", "--connect-timeout=15", "-B", "-N", "-e", q]
    out = sh(cmd).strip().splitlines()
    obj = {"_time": int(time.time()), "host": host, "cluster_name": cl, "sourcetype": "mysql:galera:wsrep"}
    for line in out:
        if not line.strip() or "\t" not in line:
            continue
        k, v = line.split("\t", 1)
        key = k.strip().lower()
        obj[key] = v.strip()
    # JSON event body: flatten wsrep_ keys + metadata
    body = json.dumps({"event": obj, "sourcetype": "mysql:galera:wsrep"}).encode("utf-8")
    req = urllib.request.Request(hec, data=body, method="POST")
    req.add_header("Authorization", f"Splunk {tok}")
    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15) as r:
        _ = r.read(200)
if __name__ == "__main__":
    try:
        main()
    except Exception as e:
        sys.stderr.write(f"{e}\n")
        sys.exit(1)
```

`inputs.conf` in the app’s `local/` (invoke the wrapper, not the bare mysql):

```ini
[script://./bin/galera_wsrep_hec.sh]
interval = 300
disabled = 0
index = openstack
sourcetype = mysql:galera:wsrep
```

`galera_wsrep_hec.sh` (mode 0755) only exports the env and calls python so secrets never print in `ps` (keep HEC in SPLUNK_HEC_URL to an HTTPS listener on a UF with `outputs.conf` or directly to a HFW in DMZ, per your standard).

`props.conf` for `[mysql:galera:wsrep]` on indexers and search heads (and HEC heavy forwarder if parsing local):

```ini
[mysql:galera:wsrep]
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
TRUNCATE = 0
INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json
KV_MODE = none
TIMESTAMP_FIELDS = _time
TIME_FORMAT = %s
```

Option B — Universal Forwarder `monitor://` of mysqld / MariaDB error log

Paths differ by packager: on Ubuntu, `/var/log/mysql/error.log`; on RHEL/Alma/Rocky, often `/var/log/mariadb/mariadb.log`. Add both stanzas; disable the one that does not exist on a given host in `local/inputs.conf` on the deployment server per OS class.

```ini
[monitor:///var/log/mysql/error.log]
disabled = 0
index = openstack
sourcetype = mysql:error_log

crcSalt = <SOURCE>

[monitor:///var/log/mariadb/mariadb.log]
disabled = 0
index = openstack
sourcetype = mariadb:error_log
crcSalt = <SOURCE>
```

`props.conf` to cut multiline backtraces at timestamps and to extract subsystems and WSREP hints:

```ini
[mysql:error_log]
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
LINE_BREAKER = ([\r\n]+)(?=(?:\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\s|InnoDB:))
TRUNCATE = 10000
MAX_TIMESTAMP_LOOKAHEAD = 25
TIME_PREFIX = ^
TIME_FORMAT = %Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S

EXTRACT-galera_sev = ^(?<mysqld_severity>\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}[^T][^:]*:)?\s*\[(?<mysqld_level>(?:Note|Warning|ERROR))\]\s+(?:(?:(?<mysqld_subsystem>[^\[:]+))\[)?(?:wsrep:)?

EXTRACT-uuid = (?<mysqld_uuid>[0-9a-fA-F]{8}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{12})

[mariadb:error_log]
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
LINE_BREAKER = ([\r\n]+)(?=(?:\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\s|InnoDB:))
TRUNCATE = 10000
TIME_FORMAT = %Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S
TIME_PREFIX = ^
# …same EXTRACT- lines as `mysql:error_log` by copying a shared `TRANSFORMS-` in transforms.conf if you want one maintenance path
```

Rationale: `TRUNCATE=10000` allows long WSREP and gcomm multi-line contexts without silent loss on bad days; the SPL still only uses pattern hit counts, not a single giant field.

Option C — SC4S syslog of mysqld (brief)

If you ship /var/lib/mysql/*.err` or mysqld from rsyslog to SC4S, use SPLUNK_COMPOSE or a vendor parsing preset if your `EVENT_SOURCETYPE_PRESET` maps the program to `syslog`. On the Splunk side, apply the same `LINE_BREAKER` and WSREP `EXTRACT-` in `props.conf` on `[syslog]` for `program` mysqld via a `TRANSFORMS-` in `transforms.conf` keyed on a `MetaData:SourceType` override, or a dedicated `sourcetype` clone like `mysqld:syslog`. The `spl` in this use case is written so `_raw` regex on `syslog` can participate when `sourcetype` is extended with an `OR` clause. Treat C as a transport convenience: keep A and B in place to avoid “syslog said healthy, but wsrep was already red” stories.

**Step 2 — Create the search and alert**

The `spl` field in this document is the saved-search body. Paste it in **Settings → Searches, reports, and alerts** → New report → Open in Search, verify rows in last 15m, and save. Then Save as → Alert.

```spl
index=openstack (sourcetype="mysql:galera:wsrep" OR sourcetype="mariadb:error_log" OR sourcetype="mysql:error_log") earliest=-15m
| eval src_kind=case(sourcetype="mysql:galera:wsrep","poll", match(sourcetype,"error_log"),"errlog", 1=1,"other")
| eval cluster_status=lower(coalesce(**wsrep_cluster_status**, cluster_status, ""))
| eval local_state=lower(coalesce(wsrep_local_state_comment, local_state_comment, ""))
| eval cluster_size=tonumber(coalesce(**wsrep_cluster_size**, cluster_size, "0"))
| eval cluster_uuid=coalesce(wsrep_cluster_state_uuid, cluster_state_uuid, "")
| eval flow_paused=tonumber(coalesce(wsrep_flow_control_paused, flow_control_paused, "0"))
| eval recv_q=tonumber(coalesce(wsrep_local_recv_queue, local_recv_queue, "0"))
| eval cluster_name=coalesce(cluster_name, "default")
| eval is_partition_log=if(src_kind="errlog" AND match(_raw, "(?i)(non-primary|partition|gcomm.*reconf|view\\(view_id|losing primary)"), 1, 0)
| eval is_donor_log=if(src_kind="errlog" AND match(_raw, "(?i)(**SST** request|requested state transfer|state transfer required|streaming changeset)"), 1, 0)
| stats latest(cluster_status) as cluster_status, latest(local_state) as local_state, latest(cluster_size) as cluster_size, latest(cluster_uuid) as cluster_uuid, max(flow_paused) as flow_paused_max, max(recv_q) as recv_q_max, sum(is_partition_log) as partition_events, sum(is_donor_log) as donor_events, max(_time) as _time by cluster_name, host
| eventstats max(cluster_size) as expected_size by cluster_name
| eventstats dc(host) as reporting_hosts by cluster_name
| eventstats dc(cluster_uuid) as uuid_count by cluster_name
| eval is_non_primary=if(cluster_status!="primary" AND cluster_status!="", 1, 0)
| eval is_size_shrunk=if(cluster_size>0 AND cluster_size<expected_size, 1, 0)
| eval is_uuid_split=if(uuid_count>1, 1, 0)
| eval is_choked=if(flow_paused_max>0.5 OR recv_q_max>200, 1, 0)
| eval in_donor_or_join=if(match(local_state, "(donor|joiner|desynced)"), 1, 0)
| eval severity_bucket=case(
    is_non_primary=1 OR is_uuid_split=1 OR (expected_size>=3 AND reporting_hosts<expected_size AND partition_events>0), "critical",
    is_size_shrunk=1 OR partition_events>=3 OR is_choked=1, "warning",
    donor_events>0 OR partition_events=1 OR in_donor_or_join=1, "watch",
    1=1, "ok")
| eval sev_order=case(severity_bucket="critical",1, severity_bucket="warning",2, severity_bucket="watch",3,1=1,4)
| sort 0 sev_order
| table _time, cluster_name, host, cluster_status, local_state, cluster_size, expected_size, reporting_hosts, partition_events, donor_events, flow_paused_max, recv_q_max, severity_bucket, sev_order
```

Pipeline read—why each stage is defensive. First, the base search is sourcetype-unioned and 15m so you catch both a poll and log spike after a blip. Second, the `coalesce` chain for `wsrep_*` and shorter aliases is how you survive a rename between TA or HEC versions without rewriting three searches. Third, the `match` on `_raw` is case-insensitive and targets gcomm/partition language that polls will never carry; you keep log noise from contaminating the OK state when the poll is still good by requiring counters in `severity_bucket`. Fourth, `stats ... by cluster_name, host` is your per-host de-dupe and time collapse; only one row per host survives so duplicate UF reads or overlapping poll retries do not sum to fake “massive” donors. Fifth, the `eventstats` trio ( `expected_size`, `reporting_hosts`, `uuid_count` ) builds in-window baselines without a separate baseline lookup for day one. Sixth, the `case` is strictly ordered so critical beats watch and you do not double-escalate; `sev_order` exists because alphabetic sort on `severity_bucket` is wrong. Seventh, the `in_donor_or_join` regex is intentionally small so Joined in a healthy string does not spam watch; tune if your site prints odd strings.

Alerting: cron `*/5 * * * *`, with `dispatch.earliest_time=-10m`, `dispatch.latest_time=now`, trigger when `severity_bucket` is `critical` or `warning`, and `alert.suppress=true` with `alert.suppress.fields=cluster_name,host` and `alert.suppress.period=15m`. For webhook bodies, pass `cluster_name`, `host`, `cluster_status`, `expected_size`, `reporting_hosts`, and `wsrep` UUID fields. Keep `watch` on-dashboard only in mature shops; in greenfield, you may route `watch` to a low-urgency Slack for training wheels.

**Step 3 — Validate**

These six checks are Galera- and index-specific; they are not the oVirt `engine.log` line-counting pattern (UC-19.1.41) and not the Ceph JSON+syslog health fusion (UC-19.1.39). Run them in staging with Option A and B both enabled, after your first 30 minutes of steady traffic.

(1) Per-host Primary sanity (poll path alive): `index=openstack sourcetype="mysql:galera:wsrep" earliest=-30m | stats count by host, wsrep_cluster_status` — every Galera member in steady state should list `wsrep_cluster_status=Primary`. If a host is missing, your scripted input is not on that node or the HEC ACL blocks it. (2) Design-size vs. observed-size: `... | stats max(wsrep_cluster_size) as observed_size, dc(host) as reporting_hosts, values(cluster_name) as cns by cluster_name` — in a healthy 3× cluster, `observed_size` should be 3 and `reporting_hosts` should be 3 for that `cluster_name`. Mismatch is either ingest hole or a real member loss. (3) State transition audit: add `| streamstats current=f last(wsrep_cluster_status) as prev_status by host | where coalesce(wsrep_cluster_status, "") != coalesce(prev_status, "")` on the same base — steady state should be no rows; any row is a state flip you can correlate to change tickets. (4) Flow-control pressure (precursor to stalls): `| stats max(wsrep_flow_control_paused) as fc_max by host` — if `fc_max` trends toward 1.0 on a hot host, the repl apply path is stalling, often a pre-partition smell. (5) Receive queue depth (slow applier / slow disk): `| stats max(wsrep_local_recv_queue) as q_max by host` — sustained q_max > 100 (site-tunable) is a straggler; pair with top disk charts. (6) UUID consistency across members: `| stats values(wsrep_cluster_state_uuid) as uuids, dc(host) as n_hosts by cluster_name | eval mv=mvcount(uuids) | where mv>1` — any `cluster_name` with more than one UUID is a split-brain or re-bootstrap in progress. This is a stop-the-line finding: do not automate bootstrap from Splunk alone. Escalate to the DBA with the host list the search prints.

**Step 4 — Tune for your environment**

Failure tolerance in `severity_bucket`: a 3-node cluster should treat any `Non-Primary` as a sev-1 database incident; a 5-node cluster may intentionally run through a one-node `Donor/Desynced` SST with quorum intact. Encode design size as `case(expected_size,3,0,5,1,0)` in a `macros.conf` macro and branch `critical` with `if` when `expected_size>=5` and exactly one straggler is in `state_comment` you trust as SST using a `maintenance` lookup. Example sketch you can paste in a child search after proof in lab: `| eval crit_alt=if(expected_size>=5 AND is_size_shrunk=1 AND is_non_primary=0, 0, is_non_primary)` and fold `crit_alt` into `case()`; keep the 3-node default strict.

Multi-cluster OpenStack estates map Kolla/TripleO service splits: control-plane Galera, cell DB, Octavia state — each gets its own `cluster_name` token in the HEC JSON. Build dashboards with `cluster_name` first column; never mix telem cluster rows with keystone cluster rows in one alert unless you are platform-wide SRE and want a fused view.

Planned drain suppression (KV lookup): a tiny `galera_planned_drains.csv` in `lookups/` with `cluster_name,host,drain_start_epoch,drain_end_epoch,change_id,owner`. At search time: `| lookup galera_planned_drains cluster_name host OUTPUT drain_start_epoch, drain_end_epoch` then `| eval in_drain=if(_time>=drain_start_epoch AND _time<=drain_end_epoch,1,0) | where in_drain=0 OR severity_bucket="critical"` — the `where` is policy: some shops still page critical through maintenance; others suppress warning only.

Gcomm reconfig language during `cluster.add`: a gcomm string during scale 3 → 5 is not the same as a partition omen when your INCM has an open change. Bind suppression to the IP list in change metadata if you can; else accept one `partition_events=1` watch in that hour.

Multi-region and WAN stretch: Galera over long RTT is delicate; if you are stretched, raise `alert.suppress.period` to 30–60m for the `warning` flaps and lengthen `earliest` to 30m to avoid WAN jitter; talk to your network team about segmented `gmcast` and intra-DC priors. SPL changes alone will not save a bad topology; this UC is honest about that—Kolla and OVN are still UC-19.1.4x siblings for Rabbit/Nova.

Capacity note: beyond 5 members, write throughput often regresses; your baseline `expected_size` of 3 or 5 is a design choice, not an accidental `max()` drift from a stray lab node—pin `expected_size` in `macros.conf` once stable.

**Step 5 — Troubleshooting**

(1) “`Primary` in mysql client but Splunk shows no poll” — the wrapper runs as splunk but `--defaults-file` is root-only, or the port in cnf points at a non-listener; fix posix perms, test `mysql` by hand as the same unix user. (2) Half Primary, half Non-Primary at the same _time in logs — treat as split-brain in motion; use Step 3 #6 `values(wsrep_cluster_state_uuid)`; escalate; eschew playbooks that auto-`pc.bootstrap=YES` without human quorum checks. (3) “All Non-Primary after a cold start” — a new bootstrap is still single-writer selection; a human must run `**galera_new_cluster**` on the chosen first node, then start joiners in the documented order; your alert is expected until the sequence finishes—tie `known_false` lookup to CAB in this window. (4) `wsrep_cluster_size` lower than 3 in steady state” — a member left `wsrep_cluster_address` gcomm:// or 4567 is filtered on one path; compare `iptables`/nft and rack-to-rack VLANs. (5) `wsrep_flow_control_paused` climbing” — slowest node drives all; find `recv_q` leader; throttle clients or re-home a hot service. (6) Error log: `WSREP: Failed to open backend connection: -110 (timed out)` once — a genuine but fleeting TCP miss; re-open if repeats; check jumbo MTU and L3 if chronic. (7) Donor Desynced for hours during SST” — not automatically bad on multi-TB; compare mariabackup or rsync logs on both ends; if disk is pegged, extend RTO expectations and your `warning` hold, not the LUN alarm at the SAN—still one chain in OpenStack depends on you finishing SST. (8) Cluster Primary but HTTP 500 everywhere in APIs” — this UC says the DB field you checked is not the story; go to HAProxy/Rabbit/Keystone fernet/Nova as separate UC-19.1.44/45 tracks; re-run DB in parallel so you are not in two Sev-1s for one HA VIP typo. (9) False positives on exact 5m tick only” — poll too short `connect-timeout`; raise to 15s or add retries=2 in the shell wrapper, and record exit code in a separate metrics file if you need SRE proof. (10) `wsrep` missing on one node after upgrade” — the plugin is not loaded; `SHOW GLOBAL VARIABLES LIKE 'wsrep%'` empty; fix `my.cnf` includes; roll back the half-upgrade if needed. (11) Primary in `SHOW GLOBAL STATUS`, but `wsrep_cluster_status=Disconnected` on a member (if you add that string variant)” — 4567/4568 path failure while local SQL still answers; the node is not in the group; this is a red not a mystic green. (12) Dashboard shows OK but operators see OpenStack CREATE timeouts” — check Haproxy timeout to Rabbit first; the db is only one leg of a 5xx; keep A+B to avoid silent gaps: mysqld can be ok with gRPC style stalls elsewhere.


## SPL

```spl
index=openstack (sourcetype="mysql:galera:wsrep" OR sourcetype="mariadb:error_log" OR sourcetype="mysql:error_log") earliest=-15m
| eval src_kind=case(sourcetype="mysql:galera:wsrep","poll", match(sourcetype,"error_log"),"errlog", 1=1,"other")
| eval cluster_status=lower(coalesce(wsrep_cluster_status, cluster_status, ""))
| eval local_state=lower(coalesce(wsrep_local_state_comment, local_state_comment, ""))
| eval cluster_size=tonumber(coalesce(wsrep_cluster_size, cluster_size, "0"))
| eval cluster_uuid=coalesce(wsrep_cluster_state_uuid, cluster_state_uuid, "")
| eval flow_paused=tonumber(coalesce(wsrep_flow_control_paused, flow_control_paused, "0"))
| eval recv_q=tonumber(coalesce(wsrep_local_recv_queue, local_recv_queue, "0"))
| eval cluster_name=coalesce(cluster_name, "default")
| eval is_partition_log=if(src_kind="errlog" AND match(_raw, "(?i)(non-primary|partition|gcomm.*reconf|view\\(view_id|losing primary)"), 1, 0)
| eval is_donor_log=if(src_kind="errlog" AND match(_raw, "(?i)(SST request|requested state transfer|state transfer required|streaming changeset)"), 1, 0)
| stats latest(cluster_status) as cluster_status, latest(local_state) as local_state, latest(cluster_size) as cluster_size, latest(cluster_uuid) as cluster_uuid, max(flow_paused) as flow_paused_max, max(recv_q) as recv_q_max, sum(is_partition_log) as partition_events, sum(is_donor_log) as donor_events, max(_time) as _time by cluster_name, host
| eventstats max(cluster_size) as expected_size by cluster_name
| eventstats dc(host) as reporting_hosts by cluster_name
| eventstats dc(cluster_uuid) as uuid_count by cluster_name
| eval is_non_primary=if(cluster_status!="primary" AND cluster_status!="", 1, 0)
| eval is_size_shrunk=if(cluster_size>0 AND cluster_size<expected_size, 1, 0)
| eval is_uuid_split=if(uuid_count>1, 1, 0)
| eval is_choked=if(flow_paused_max>0.5 OR recv_q_max>200, 1, 0)
| eval in_donor_or_join=if(match(local_state, "(donor|joiner|desynced)"), 1, 0)
| eval severity_bucket=case(
    is_non_primary=1 OR is_uuid_split=1 OR (expected_size>=3 AND reporting_hosts<expected_size AND partition_events>0), "critical",
    is_size_shrunk=1 OR partition_events>=3 OR is_choked=1, "warning",
    donor_events>0 OR partition_events=1 OR in_donor_or_join=1, "watch",
    1=1, "ok")
| eval sev_order=case(severity_bucket="critical",1, severity_bucket="warning",2, severity_bucket="watch",3,1=1,4)
| sort 0 sev_order
| table _time, cluster_name, host, cluster_status, local_state, cluster_size, expected_size, reporting_hosts, partition_events, donor_events, flow_paused_max, recv_q_max, severity_bucket, sev_order
```

## Visualization

Row A — **Single value** for worst `sev_order` in last 15m (optionally by `cluster_name`). Row B — **Timechart** of `flow_paused_max` and `recv_q_max` by `host` to catch **choked** appliers. Row C — **Table** with `cluster_name, host, cluster_status, expected_size, reporting_hosts, partition_events, donor_events, severity_bucket`, sorted by `sev_order` then `cluster_name`. Row D — **Timechart** of `partition_events` and `donor_events` (sum) per 5m for **log** cadence. Drilldown: raw **`mysql:galera:wsrep` JSON** and **error_log** for the `host` and minute.

## Known False Positives

Planned changes to `wsrep_provider_options` and short **gcomm** reconfig windows during a documented **3→5** node **scale-up**, where the cluster is still **writeable** and **CAB** has an open change. A **first** **bootstrap** node that is **intentionally** **Non-Primary** until **`cluster.add`** of joiners completes during **greenfield** build. A **rolling** **MariaDB** minor **restart** in **maintenance** with a **staged** `systemctl restart` order that still preserves **quorum** on the others. A **Donor/Desynced** donor during a **known** long **SST** for a **new** or **re-imaged** member—pair with a **time-bounded** change record before paging. A **fast** **IST** catch-up after a **brief** **network** flap—often **no** `Non-Primary` in **polls**, only a **one-line** gcomm string in the **errlog** (watch, not sev-1). A **stretched** but **valid** **WAN** test where no **write** is accepted and **all** members agree on **read-only**—a **gameday** in **UAT/QA** that is **intentional** so DBAs can rehearse **pc.bootstrap=YES**—suppress by **index**=lab. **MaxScale/ProxySQL** or **Haproxy** **reloads** that **churn** client connections, surfacing as **mysqld** notes without a **quorum** break. A **mysqld-only** process restart to **roll** `error` logs or **reclaim** memory in a way that is **not** a **wsrep** leave—rare, but the **errlog** can **spike** **Note**/**Warning** that this UC will rate as **low** if **polls** stay **Primary**. A **mismatch** in **naming** of **`wsrep_sst_method`** (e.g. **switch** from **mysqldump** to **mariabackup**) that prints scary **SST** lines but finishes **on schedule**. A **one-off** **false** **“partition”** word in a **user**-generated **query** line accidentally logged—use **CIM**-style allowlists in **`props.conf`** to drop **app**-level SQL text if you have that risk.
